hook.text: Three generations under one roof: grandparents, parents, children, and cousins. In much of the world, this is normal.

buildup.text: Extended families include multiple generations and relatives living together or in close proximity, sharing resources.

discovery.text: They provide built-in childcare, elder care, economic support, and social insurance that the nuclear family lacks.

twist.text: Extended families can be restrictive. Freedom, privacy, and personal choice are often sacrificed for the group.

climax.text: Extended families trade individual freedom for collective security. They're resilient but demand conformity.

punchline.text: More family means more support and more rules.

quiz.question: What advantage do extended families provide?
options: Built-in childcare, elder care, and economic support
